Medium Armor.special: Wearing Scale Mail or Half Plate imposes Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks.

Heavy Armor.calculation: Flat AC provided by the armor (no Dex modifier)
Heavy Armor.special: Wearing Ring Mail, Chain Mail, Splint, or Plate imposes Disadvantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks. Speed is reduced by 10 feet if Strength requirement is not met.

Simple Weapons.description: Standard weapons requiring no specialized martial training[cite: 4].

Martial Weapons.description: Advanced weapons requiring specific class proficiencies to wield effectively[cite: 4].

Finesse.effect: Choose either Strength or Dexterity modifier for attack and damage rolls.

Heavy.effect: Small creatures (e.g., Halflings, Gnomes) have Disadvantage on attack rolls.

Thrown.effect: Uses the same ability modifier for attack and damage that you would use for a melee attack.

Available Packs.description: Pre-bundled sets of adventuring gear granted during character creation based on class and background[cite: 4].

General Tool Rule.benefit: Add Proficiency Bonus to any ability check made using a tool, kit, or instrument you are proficient with.

Musical Instruments.benefit: Used for performances and can act as a spellcasting focus for Bards[cite: 4].

Artisan's Tools.benefit: Used for crafting and specific trade skills[cite: 4].

Thieves' Tools.benefit: Required for picking locks and disarming traps[cite: 4].

Herbalism Kit.benefit: Required to craft antitoxins or Potions of Healing[cite: 4].

Healer's Kit.benefit: Contains 10 uses; expend 1 use to instantly stabilize a creature at 0 HP without a Medicine check.

Arcane Focus.description: A crystal, orb, rod, or wand used to channel magic for Sorcerers, Warlocks, and Wizards[cite: 4].

Druidic Focus.description: A sprig of mistletoe, totem, or wooden staff used specifically by Druids[cite: 4].

Holy Symbol.description: An amulet or emblem used to channel divine magic for Clerics and Paladins[cite: 4].

Component Pouch.description: A pouch holding physical materials required for casting spells, serving as an alternative to a spellcasting focus[cite: 4].

Spellbook.description: A critical item for Wizards to record, store, and prepare their spells[cite: 4].

Crowbar.benefit: Advantage on Strength checks where leverage can be applied.

Portable Ram.benefit: +4 bonus to Strength checks to break down doors; Advantage if assisted by another character.

Magnifying Glass.benefit: Advantage on ability checks to appraise or inspect small or highly detailed items.

Block and Tackle.benefit: Allows you to hoist up to four times your normal lifting weight.

Base College Package.benefit: All students receive a school uniform, writing supplies, 10gp, and their college-specific textbook[cite: 3].

Star Performer Kit.benefit: Contains custom instruments, premium paints/inks, and performance supplies[cite: 3].

Stealthy Archivist Kit.benefit: Contains a hooded lantern, steel mirror, and durable backpack[cite: 3].

Practical Survivalist Kit.benefit: Contains tents, ropes, field rations, and emergency gear[cite: 3].

College Primers.benefit: Holds 3 charges. Expend 1 charge for a 1d4 bonus to college-tied skill checks. Allows casting of one 1st-level class spell once per long rest without a slot.

Cuddly Strixhaven Mascot.benefit: Can be used to automatically succeed on a failed saving throw to avoid the frightened condition.

The Student Die.benefit: Grants a 1d4 bonus die to specific skill checks tied to your campus club or job[cite: 3].

Strixhaven Pennant.benefit: Sheds bright light in a 10-foot radius and dim light for an additional 10 feet when waved.

Bottle of Boundless Coffee.benefit: Provides coffee that is always comfortably warm and never runs out.
